supply_chain.dependencies: Fabless business model - relies entirely on third-party foundries for wafer manufacturing and third-party subcontractors for assembly and testing. Significant concentration in Asia, particularly China. Proprietary process technologies installed on third-party equipment.
supply_chain.risks: Concentration of manufacturing and testing facilities in China exposes company to political, regulatory, economic, foreign exchange, and operational risks. Dependence on limited number of suppliers for wafer purchases. Potential capacity constraints from suppliers. Natural disasters (earthquakes, flooding, heatwaves) in Asia could disrupt operations. Supply chain lead times of 16-26 weeks create inventory forecasting challenges.

regulatory_risks: U.S. export control laws and regulations (Export Administration Act, Export Administration Regulations) restricting technology transfers and sales to certain countries
International trade policies and tariffs - particularly U.S.-China tariffs and potential retaliatory measures
U.S. Uyghur Forced Labor Prevention Act - restrictions on goods from regions with forced labor concerns
China Cyber Security Law - requirements for data collection, use, storage, disclosure and security
China Data Security Law - regulations on data processing and cross-border transfers
China Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL) - controls on personal data use and cross-border transfers with potential fines
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) - data protection requirements with significant fines for non-compliance
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act - state-level data privacy requirements
Environmental regulations on product substances, recycling, and disposal (various EU and Asia jurisdictions)
ESG reporting requirements - California greenhouse gas emissions disclosure laws (enacted 2023-2024)
Supply chain compliance - conflict minerals regulations and human rights due diligence
Section 162(m) of Internal Revenue Code - tax deduction limitations on executive compensation
Bermuda Corporate Income Tax Act of 2023 - 15% corporate income tax for MNE groups effective 2025
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax framework (15%) - implementation varies by jurisdiction
Potential AI technology export restrictions and regulations
Import/export licensing requirements for certain products and technologies
Changing tax regulations in China affecting operations in Chengdu, Hangzhou and other regions

sanction_risks: U.S. sanctions and export controls on Russia due to Ukraine conflict
Potential sanctions or restrictions related to Hong Kong due to Chinese government laws
U.S.-China trade tensions and potential for expanded export restrictions
Risk of being prohibited from selling to foreign customers requiring export licenses
Economic sanctions that could limit export of products and services
Restrictions on AI-enabling technologies and products
